message:
fix(autograd,core,serialize): add checked arithmetic for shape volumes to prevent overflow and canonicalize checkpoint entry order

Shape overflow safety (ft-api, ft-autograd, ft-core):
Replace all unchecked `shape.iter().product()` calls with
`checked_shape_numel()` which uses `checked_mul` and short-circuits on
zero dimensions. This prevents silent wraparound when adversarial or
malformed tensor shapes contain very large dimension values (e.g.,
`[usize::MAX, usize::MAX]`), which would previously wrap to a small
number and cause incorrect memory allocations or out-of-bounds access.

Affected operations: reshape, unflatten, flatten, permute, flip,
repeat, roll, median, and backward passes for transpose/permute/repeat.

The `permute_data` helper now returns `Result<Vec<f64>, AutogradError>`
instead of `Vec<f64>` to propagate overflow errors from numel computation
rather than silently producing incorrect results.

Also add `checked_mul_usize()` helper for dimension-by-repeat
multiplication in repeat operations, preventing shape[d]*repeats[d]
overflow from producing undersized output tensors.

ft-core `numel()` now short-circuits to 0 when any dimension is zero,
preventing the zero from being reached only after an earlier overflow
returns usize::MAX.

Checkpoint canonicalization (ft-serialize):
Both strict and hardened checkpoint decode paths now call
`normalize_entries()` to sort entries by node_id after deserialization.
This ensures deterministic entry ordering regardless of how the checkpoint
was serialized, preventing subtle comparison/diff failures when the same
checkpoint is written by different code paths.

New tests:
- Overflow rejection for unflatten, reshape, repeat (ft-api)
- Zero-dimension short-circuit before overflow (ft-core)
- Entry order canonicalization for strict and hardened decode (ft-serialize)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
